messages.content: powershell remove all rows from csv file with missing email address

As an AI language model, I cannot perform actions on files or execute codes on a device. However, I can provide you with the PowerShell code that can remove all rows from a CSV file with missing email addresses. Here is the code:

```
# Replace 'file.csv' with the name and path of your CSV file
$csv = Import-Csv -Path 'file.csv'

# Filter out rows with missing email address
$filtered = $csv | Where-Object { $_.Email -ne $null }

# Replace 'filtered-file.csv' with the name and path of the filtered CSV file
$filtered | Export-Csv -Path 'filtered-file.csv' -NoTypeInformation
```

This code imports the CSV file using the `Import-Csv` cmdlet, then filters out rows that have a missing email address using the `Where-Object` cmdlet. Finally, the filtered data is exported to a new CSV file using the `Export-Csv` cmdlet.
want results in same file

To overwrite the original CSV file with the filtered data, you can modify the last line of the code as follows:

```
# Replace 'file.csv' with the name and path of your CSV file
$csv = Import-Csv -Path 'file.csv'

# Filter out rows with missing email address
$filtered = $csv | Where-Object { $_.Email -ne $null }

# Overwrite the original CSV file with the filtered data
$filtered | Export-Csv -Path 'file.csv' -NoTypeInformation
```

This code imports the CSV file, filters out rows with missing email addresses, and overwrites the original CSV file with the filtered data using the same filename. Be aware that this will permanently modify the original file, so make sure to have a backup beforehand if needed.
